commands.review_summary:
The PR enables second-precision envelope timestamps in core auto-reply formatting, aligns the plugin channel test helper, and updates focused tests plus timestamp docs.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests 0, Docs 0. Total +4 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows envelope timestamp formatting omits displaySeconds, and current tests expect minute-only inbound metadata output.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the patch is focused and I found no actionable defect for automation to fix.

target.title: fix(inbound-meta): include seconds in timestamps
target.body:
## Summary
- Include seconds in inbound metadata envelope timestamps while preserving UTC, local, and configured timezone handling.
- Update the shared plugin test helper so extension tests expect the same timestamp precision.
- Add focused regression coverage for second-precision metadata timestamps, including inbound metadata expectations.

Fixes #87257

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/envelope.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<generated include file> OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=auto-reply-reply-state-routing OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts -t "applies WhatsApp group reset authorization"`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/envelope.ts src/auto-reply/envelope.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/test-helpers/envelope-timestamp.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.connection-and-logging.e2e.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Inbound metadata timestamps now include second precision instead of stopping at minutes.
Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout rebased onto the latest upstream main before pushing this update.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the verification commands listed above and captured inbound metadata output from the runtime formatter path with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`.
Evidence after fix: The after-fix terminal capture below shows the inbound metadata formatter path returning timestamps with non-zero seconds for both UTC and configured user timezone output.
Observed result after fix: Targeted tests, the matching CI shard, the WhatsApp helper e2e test, and formatting checks passed locally.
What was not tested: Full repository test suite was not run.

After-fix terminal capture:
```text
[after-fix terminal capture] inbound metadata timestamps
utc=Sun 2026-02-15T13:35:42Z
userTimezone=Thu 2026-03-19 09:00:27 GMT+9
```
target.author: garlicgo
